findings.title: Development TLS private keys tracked in git
findings.impact: Development TLS keys are tracked in version control. While these are dev-only keys, tracking private keys is discouraged.
findings.remediation:
1) Add configs/tls/dev/*.key.pem to .gitignore
2) Document key generation in README
3) Generate keys locally during dev setup

findings.title: GitHub Actions not pinned to commit SHA
findings.impact: Actions pinned to mutable tags (@v4, @main) are vulnerable to supply chain attacks if action maintainers' credentials are compromised.
findings.remediation:
Pin all actions to full 40-character SHA:
uses: actions/checkout@v4 → uses: actions/checkout@a12b3c4d5e6f...
Use dependabot or renovate to auto-update SHAs with comments showing tag equivalents.

findings.title: Bandit MEDIUM severity issues

samples.issue: Audit url open for permitted schemes. Allowing use of file:/ or custom schemes is often unexpected.
Audit url open for permitted schemes. Allowing use of file:/ or custom schemes is often unexpected.
Probable insecure usage of temp file/directory.

findings.impact: Medium severity code security issues detected by Bandit static analyzer
findings.remediation: Review each finding in audit/artifacts/bandit.json and address legitimate security concerns
findings.verification: bandit -r src backtest scripts -ll  # Should show reduced count

findings.title: Subprocess module usage without input validation
findings.impact: Scripts use subprocess calls. While no shell=True found, review for untrusted input.
findings.remediation:
1) Audit all subprocess calls for user-controlled input
2) Prefer pathlib/stdlib over subprocess when possible
3) Use explicit argument lists, not string commands
findings.verification: Review scripts with subprocess usage and add input validation where needed

findings.title: Assert statements in production code

evidence.detail: B101: Assert statements will be removed with python -O

findings.impact: Assert statements are removed when Python runs in optimized mode (-O). Code should not rely on asserts for validation.
findings.remediation: For user input validation, use explicit if/raise. Asserts are OK for developer sanity checks.
findings.verification: Review critical validation logic and replace asserts with explicit checks where needed

findings.title: Try-except-pass patterns suppress errors
findings.impact: Silent error suppression can hide bugs and security issues
findings.remediation: Replace except-pass with explicit handling or at minimum log the exception

findings.title: ✅ No production secrets in repository

evidence.detail: All detected secrets are false positives (test fixtures, docs examples, .secrets.baseline)

findings.impact: POSITIVE: No production secrets detected in current tree or git history
findings.remediation: Maintain current practice: use .env files, secret managers, never commit real credentials

findings.title: ✅ Excellent supply chain security

evidence.detail: Lock files present, 0 vulnerabilities, SBOM generated

findings.impact: POSITIVE: Strong dependency management with lock files and vulnerability scanning
findings.remediation: Continue current practices. Consider OSV scanner in CI for additional coverage.

findings.title: ✅ Comprehensive security CI workflows
findings.impact: POSITIVE: Repository has extensive security automation including SLSA provenance, SBOM generation, and policy enforcement
findings.remediation: Continue maintaining these workflows. Ensure they run on all PRs.

findings.title: .secrets.baseline may contain outdated entries
findings.impact: The .secrets.baseline file is large (131 entries). Periodically review to ensure entries are still necessary.
findings.remediation:
1) Regenerate baseline: detect-secrets scan > .secrets.baseline
2) Audit and remove entries for deleted files
3) Document baseline maintenance in CONTRIBUTING.md
